title: Bash argparse manifest
description:
Declarative YAML/JSON manifest for generic bash argument parsing, validation, URI resolution, and --help generation. Consumed by argparse__parse_args() in lib/argparse.sh.

Usage text is never stored in the manifest; the runtime library generates --help from script.* and options[]. The Usage line is derived as '<script.name> [OPTIONS]' (or the script basename from $0 when more accurate).

For each option, var and flag are optional. When omitted, the runtime derives them from name: var = UPPER(name) with hyphens mapped to underscores; flag = '--' + name.

Input resolution modes control whether values come from CLI flags, environment variables, or a combination. Global input.mode applies to all options unless overridden per option via options[].input.mode.

Post-parse pipeline order (when enabled): set log level → static defaults → dynamic defaults → normalize arrays → resolve URIs → validate → unexport option variables.

schema_version.description: Manifest format version. Bump when breaking runtime or jq filter contracts.

id.description: Optional manifest identifier for logging and debugging.

script.description: Program identity and help text shown above the Options section. The runtime never stores a pre-rendered Usage line; it builds --help from these fields plus options[].

name.description: Program or script display name (first line of --help).

version.description: Version string appended to the display name in --help.

summary.description: One-line summary printed under the title.

description.description: Longer description printed before the Options section.

input.description: Global CLI/environment input policy. Applies to every option unless an option declares its own input.mode override.

mode.description: Default input resolution mode for all options.

env_trigger.description:
Used only when mode is env_exclusive_else_cli. Selects when the runtime takes the environment branch instead of parsing CLI flags.

any_option: at least one tracked option variable is set (${VAR+defined}).

all_options: every tracked option variable is set.

listed: at least one variable named in env_trigger_vars is set.

env_trigger_vars.description: Bash variable names consulted when env_trigger is listed. Required in that case.

allOf.description: When env_trigger is listed, env_trigger_vars must name at least one variable.

pipeline.description: Post-parse processing toggles. Omitted keys use the documented defaults. Steps run in fixed order when enabled: set log level → static defaults → dynamic defaults → normalize arrays → resolve URIs → validate → unexport option variables.

set_log_level.description: After parsing, call logging__set_level when a LOG_LEVEL (or equivalent) option is present.

defaults.description: Apply static default and default_dynamic expressions for options that declare them.

normalize_arrays.description: Trim array elements and drop blank entries before validation.

resolve_uri.description: Resolve uri-marked option values to local filesystem paths.

validate.description: Run type, enum, integer range, and path_tests validations.

unexport_options.description: Mark all option variables (scalars and arrays) non-exported (declare -g +x) so parsed values are not inherited by child processes. Especially important for env-sourced array options, which can become exported during newline splitting.

options.description: Ordered list of CLI/environment options. Order determines --help column order and stable parse iteration. Each options[].name must be unique; each options[].var must be unique when explicitly set or after default derivation.

inputMode.description:
How option values are read from CLI flags and/or environment variables.

cli_exclusive_else_env: if argv is non-empty, parse CLI only; otherwise read env only.

env_exclusive_else_cli: if env_trigger matches, read env only; otherwise parse CLI only.

cli_only: ignore env for option values.

env_only: ignore CLI except -h|--help.

cli_then_env: parse CLI, then fill unset options from env.

env_then_cli: read env, then let CLI override per option.

bashVarName.description: Bash environment variable name in UPPER_SNAKE_CASE. When omitted on an option, the runtime derives it from name by uppercasing and mapping hyphens to underscores.

optionEntry.description: One option declaration. Defines how the runtime exposes a value on the CLI (--flag), in the environment (var), and in generated --help text. Type-specific properties and default shapes are constrained by the conditional rules in allOf.

name.description: Canonical option identifier (lower_snake or kebab-case). Drives default var and flag when those are omitted.

var.description: Optional override for the bash variable name. Default: derived from name.

flag.description: Optional override for the long-only CLI flag. Default: '--' + name.

type.description: Value type for parsing, validation, and --help formatting. Determines which companion properties are allowed.

description.description: Full option description. Runtime --help uses the first sentence; the remainder may appear in extended help.

input.description: Optional per-option override of the manifest-level input.mode. Omit this property entirely to inherit input.mode from the manifest root.

mode.description: Input resolution mode for this option only.

skip_static_default.description: When true, skip static default and default_dynamic application for this option. Use when an external codegen step or resolver must supply the value instead.

default.description: Static default applied when the user omits the option. Omitted property means no static default: scalars initialize to empty string during CLI parsing; arrays stay unset until env/CLI provides a value. The allowed JSON type depends on options[].type (see allOf rules).

default_dynamic.description: Shell expression used as the assignment right-hand side when the variable is still empty after static default application. Evaluated by the runtime defaults pipeline step.

enum.description: Allowed values enforced at validation time. Empty string is a valid enum member.

proposals.description: Suggested values shown in --help only; not validated at runtime.

items.description: One suggested value with explanatory text for --help.

description.description: Short explanation shown next to the suggestion in --help.

minimum.description: Inclusive lower bound for integer options. May appear together with maximum.

maximum.description: Inclusive upper bound for integer options. Should be greater than or equal to minimum (also enforced by runtime tests).

path_tests.description: Bash unary path tests applied after URI resolution. Each test is checked independently; empty option values skip validation.

items.description: Bash unary test operator passed to argparse__validate_path or argparse__validate_path_array. Examples: -f, -d, ! -e.

uri.description: When present, the option value may be a URI that the runtime resolves to a local path before path_tests run. Use true for default behavior or an object for fetch-time chmod settings.

oneOf.description: Enable URI resolution with default materialization behavior.
URI resolution with optional post-fetch file mode.

chmod.description: chmod mode applied after materializing fetched content (for example 600 or +x). Not applied when the value already refers to an existing local path.

allOf.description: boolean options may only use boolean defaults and must not declare enum, integer bounds, path_tests, or uri.

default.description: Default boolean value when the flag is omitted.

allOf.description: integer options may use integer defaults and optional minimum/maximum bounds; enum, path_tests, and uri are not allowed.

default.description: Default integer value as a JSON number or decimal string.

allOf.description: When minimum and maximum are both set on an integer option, both must be integers. Runtime and codegen additionally require maximum >= minimum.
string options may use string defaults and optional enum, path_tests, and uri; integer bounds are not allowed.

default.description: Default string value when the option is omitted.

allOf.description: array options accept repeatable CLI flags and env values; default is a string (empty means empty array, otherwise newline-delimited elements). Integer bounds are not allowed.

default.description: Empty string means an empty array. A non-empty string is treated as newline-delimited default elements.
